variables.name: Leading-edge logic fabrication (sub-5nm) + Taiwan concentration

actor_readings.reading: China's minimum stave for FRONTIER TRAINING (5nm-class dense clusters), ~4yr behind with a finite window; inference/deployment run fine on mature nodes (#109, #110, #111). Medium-high confidence.

Not gated on chip capability but exposed to the Taiwan SPOF: a blockade/strike halts global frontier hardware 2-5yr with <15% substitution (#5). High confidence on exposure, low on trigger probability.

variables.current_reading: US/allied-controlled and Taiwan-concentrated: TSMC >90% of sub-5nm, a single point of failure priced near zero (#5, #118). China is ~4yr behind, not 10 (#110): SMIC 7nm via DUV multipatterning, 5nm targeted 2027-28 at <50% yield; indigenous EUV in trial (#110, #125). High confidence on the concentration fact; medium on China's exact gap.
variables.trajectory: Slow-clearing and hardest to catch up: no greenfield leading-edge entrant since TSMC 1987 (#74). Process-recipe tacit knowledge is the non-transferable core (#125, A38 Zeiss, A55 Nikon, A29 TSMC=~14yr even under favorable conditions). TSMC Arizona reaches only ~20% by 2030 (#118, #144). Manufacturing catch-up historically imitable (A13/A14/A28/A51) BUT EUV-denial breaks every LCD/POSCO/Airbus analogy at the leading edge (A24/A28/A34/A52).
variables.base_rate: East-Asian fab catch-up took 12-18yr WITH equipment access (A28, A32, A37). Base rate for greenfield leading-edge under active tool denial is effectively zero (#74). Analogy breaks: prior catch-ups all had a 'Kawasaki moment' of licensed transfer that export controls now block.

variables.name: Power & grid execution (electrons, transformers, interconnection)

actor_readings.reading: This IS the US minimum stave — grid interconnection + transformers + parts (#106, #108). High confidence.
Not power-, speed-, or equipment-gated; controls the grid-equipment supply chain that also taxes the US (#107, #132, A53). High confidence.
Structural energy advantage: sub-$15/MWh solar, greenfield grid, no PUC/queue (#147) — potentially 40-60% below US all-in cost. Medium confidence this converts to swing-supplier status by 2030.

variables.current_reading: Execution-gated for the US, decisively (#3, #7, #106): FERC queue >2,700GW with 5-7yr waits (#17), PJM power +76% YoY, HV transformer lead times 2.5-4yr (#106, #132), 30-50% of planned 2026 US datacenters delayed/cancelled on power/parts. Capital is NOT the constraint (#105). High confidence.
variables.trajectory: Slow-clearing for the US (5-15yr generation, 10yr transmission #7) but not permanent: nuclear restarts bankable near-term (#134), behind-the-meter routes locally (~8.9GW vs 44GW gap #106), SMRs 2030+ (#16), permitting reform is the swing. China faces essentially no version (#107: +429GW 2024 vs US +49GW). PUC friction (#58-#68) is a durable domestic drag independent of physics.
variables.base_rate: Energy-intensive infra runs 2-4x over on cost/schedule, execution- not capital-gated (A5 nuclear, A6 Panama 'prior constraint', A3 electrification 65yr). Analogy breaks: no single catastrophic-failure regulator; distributed not single-project. Alcoa/electrification (A8/A3) show energy geography is deterministic and durable.

variables.name: Advanced packaging (CoWoS) + HBM
variables.current_reading: Currently the binding near-term chip sub-constraint, more than wafer starts (#14, #115): TSMC CoWoS ~85-90%, HBM oligopoly SK Hynix-dominant (#15). Where H100/B200/GB200 supply actually chokes. High confidence.
variables.trajectory: Medium-clearing: packaging is closer to the 'catchable' end (A51 batteries, A54 shipbuilding commodity segments; JCET/Tianshui competitive at mature packaging). HBM is historically catchable — memory displaced ~every 12-15yr (#137), CXMT ~2-3yr behind on HBM3 (#115). Faster than leading-edge logic, slower than software.
variables.base_rate: Memory/packaging catch-up ~12-15yr per generational displacement (A137 US→Japan→Korea DRAM), and memory IS catchable unlike leading-edge logic because process complexity is lower. Analogy holds better here than for V1.

variables.name: Critical materials & grid-equipment refining (China counter-lever)

actor_readings.reading: China's primary OFFENSIVE lever on the West's physical buildout — controls the input most proximate to grid interconnection (transformers, #132). High confidence.
A real cost/timeline tax on the buildout, biting earlier than the fab layer; unaddressed by CHIPS-style policy (#25, #113). Medium-high confidence.

variables.current_reading: China-dominant and already weaponized (#113, #25): rare earths ~87-90%, gallium ~98%, germanium ~68%, magnets ~90% (tier-3 fabrication is the true bottleneck, #141), plus ~70-80% of large power transformers (#132). Escalation ladder run 2023-2025 with 3-6x price spikes. High confidence.
variables.trajectory: Soft but durable lever (#114): raises Western buildout cost +15-25% and timeline +6-12mo, a tax not a knockout — reversible short-run (embargoes lasted months: A16 2010 rare-earths, A19 OPEC). Western reshoring reaches only ~20-25% of China output by 2028 (#114). Refining is the stickiest layer (A22 Standard Oil, A16). Substitution feasible on 5-10yr peacetime clock (A30 synthetic rubber, A33 Haber-Bosch).
variables.base_rate: Concentrated-refining chokepoints stickier than fungible-commodity embargoes: diversification took a decade and never broke China's refining share (A16) vs months for oil (A19). Japan specialty chemicals (#119) and litho gases (#120) are distinct mutual materials chokepoints affecting all fabs symmetrically.

variables.name: EUV lithography / semiconductor tooling chokepoint

actor_readings.reading: The gate behind the gate — what actually gates V1 leading-edge. Eroding on a self-set clock plus demand-shift (#111). Medium-high confidence.
Netherlands (ASML) + Germany (Zeiss) hold a de facto veto the US cannot exercise unilaterally (#121). High confidence on the structural fact.

variables.current_reading: US-allied single-source and the hardest, most durable chokepoint in the stack (#112 ranking: EUV > fabs > HBM > design > packaging > EDA). ASML ~98% of sub-5nm, Zeiss optics sub-monopoly makes Germany an unacknowledged veto actor (#121). High confidence.
variables.trajectory: Hardest to erode but finite window (~2026-28): China ~4yr behind with indigenous LDP-EUV in trial at ~50% throughput (#110). Optics ecosystem resisted even forcible full transfer (A38 Zeiss: >decade after deporting the engineers). Controls buy years not denial (A15 COCOM 'slow not stop', A23 Siberian pipeline collapsed in 11mo, A35 Slater talent-vector). Lever also loses value as demand shifts from frontier-5nm (#111).
variables.base_rate: Multilateral tech-denial regimes have a ~15-20yr half-life (A133 COCOM) BUT modern trilateral held tighter than COCOM (A15), and dollar-clearing/OFAC make defection structurally harder (#142). Base rate for replicating a distributed precision-optics ecosystem: 15-25yr (A38).

variables.name: Software & platform moat (CUDA / EDA / cluster interconnect)

actor_readings.reading: A durable, geography-independent rent layer capturing value regardless of where fabs/datacenters sit (#27, #53). But EDA reversal shows lobbyable layers are weak (#112). Medium-high confidence.
Escaping via RISC-V (#127) and forced CANN indigenization; achieves domestic sufficiency, not global displacement (A80 bifurcation). Medium confidence.

variables.current_reading: US-durable at CUDA + interconnect (NVLink/InfiniBand, #122), but layer-by-layer variance: EDA controls already REVERSED July 2025 (#112, weak chokepoint), while CUDA/interconnect remain sticky. China's software stacks are fragmented (CANN/Cambricon/Kunlunxin), the worst outcome (#79 HEVC failure mode). Medium-high confidence.
variables.trajectory: Software-interface moats erode faster than physical/manufacturing moats (A20 Wintel→ARM; #112). But historically 10-15yr minimum to displace a hardware-embedded standard (A79 H264→AV1, A80 TCP/IP vs OSI), and government mandates fail to displace de-facto standards (A80 GOSIP). CUDA likely bifurcates (China CANN walled-garden) rather than displaced globally. RISC-V/CPO/open weights are erosion vectors (#127, #135, #145).
variables.base_rate: Ecosystem/workflow moats persist 20-45yr against cheaper technically-equal rivals absent a forcing function (A75 System/360, A76 Oracle, A78 Bloomberg). Displacement comes from ARCHITECTURAL shift not cloning (A78, A49 vacuum-tube→transistor). Bifurcation (A80) is the likeliest outcome, not global replacement.

variables.name: Frontier AI talent

actor_readings.reading: A candidate US minimum stave for long-run frontier leadership — the one constraint the US inflicts on itself via visa policy (#83, #84). India is the absent swing that could relieve it (#131). Medium confidence.
Retention + returnees strengthen China where knowledge is tacit (algorithms), but binding fab infra is missing to deploy process-talent (#89, A64). Medium confidence.
Talent-repelling via GDPR research-data friction independent of pay (#90). Medium confidence.

variables.current_reading: US-concentrated but self-sabotaging: >50% of US AI PhDs foreign-born, power-law concentration in a few hundred researchers (#82), yet H-1B caps / green-card backlogs act as adversarial de-facto industrial policy (#83). Return migration of Chinese-origin researchers accelerating (#85). Equity comp is a genuine US retention moat SOEs/Gulf can't match (#86). Medium confidence.
variables.trajectory: Structurally inelastic — a 5-7yr PhD pipeline capital cannot accelerate (#81), slower-clearing than power or financing in the medium term. Diaspora repatriation works only where domestic infra exists to deploy it (A65 Taiwan sea-turtle YES; A66 Russia/Skolkovo NO; A64 Paperclip: fast where knowledge is tacit + base exists). US institutional pull more durable than any restriction regime (A67 India IIT).
variables.base_rate: A single-decade talent shock can shift frontier capability irreversibly (A63 Nazi émigrés → 50yr US dominance). Knowledge-layer catch-up is non-imitable unlike manufacturing (#77); AI architecture closer to tacit-embodied (A63/A68 Fuchs) than published-engineering. Analogy breaks: AI publishes openly, lowering the 'Fuchs premium' vs 1930s physics.

variables.name: Capital structure & financial fragility

actor_readings.reading: Capital is NOT the US constraint (#105); fragility is a tier-specific financial-market risk, not a national buildout stave. High confidence.
Sub-market sovereign capital sustains buildout past private-market signals (#18), suppressing clearing — but faces landlord-not-owner value-capture risk (A87-A92). Medium confidence.

variables.current_reading: Bifurcated by tier: hyperscalers self-fund from $50B+/yr FCF (#41, ~$725B 2026 capex #105) and are insulated; fragility concentrates at neocloud/SPV tier — GPU-as-collateral (#36/#37), circular vendor financing (#35), take-or-pay operating leverage (#43), VC-dependent customers (#40), off-balance-sheet SPVs (#45). Medium-high confidence.
variables.trajectory: Fast-transmitting at the fragile tier (impairment within quarters, #40) but slow at the core. GAAP 4-5yr depreciation vs 18-24mo competitive life is a hidden write-down (#38, #39). Political economy may block write-downs for subsidized/national-security assets, inverting the fiber bust (#31). Sovereign/defense/deterrence demand floors (#29, #54) and Gulf sub-market capital (#18) delay clearing.
variables.base_rate: Front-loaded infra capex on uncertain demand → write-downs not moats (A4 fiber 97% dark, A1 railroad Panic 1893 bankrupted 1/3). Analogy breaks: 2000-era CLECs were leveraged; today's dominant buyers are not (#41) — a bust craters neoclouds without halting hyperscaler buildout. ZIRP-era hurdle rates never repriced (#44).

variables.name: Model-layer value & open-weight commoditization

actor_readings.reading: Adversarial open-sourcing is a rational asymmetric response — commoditize US closed-model rents where China can't match silicon (#101, #96). Relieves China's chip constraint mainly for inference (#116). Medium confidence.
Open-weight/API leakage undermines the export-control chokepoint (#96, #140, #143) and the frontier-lab rent thesis; a legal gap BIS cannot quickly close (#140). Medium confidence.

variables.current_reading: Commoditization is LIVE, not speculative (#99): open weights (Llama/Qwen/DeepSeek) cover most enterprise use cases; DeepSeek R1 ~$5-6M refutes capex-justification (#93); distillation compresses the frontier premium faster than the buildout amortizes (#97). Inference-time-compute scaling shifts demand from centralized training to distributed inference (#95, #103). Medium-high confidence, high uncertainty on durability.
variables.trajectory: Fastest-diffusing layer — frontier weights diffuse in weeks (A17 Soviet A-bomb ~4yr for the HARD case; A48 AlexNet at internet speed). Zero marginal replication cost undermines infrastructure-rent thesis (#100). Jevons rebound is the counter-mechanism: efficiency expands total compute demand (#47, #116, A25 shale, A26 Green Revolution, A48). Whether efficiency destroys or expands demand is genuinely unresolved.
variables.base_rate: Value migrates AWAY from the physical/manufacturing layer to platform/reorg (A13 Japan-in-software, A39 Bell Labs, A40 Nokia→Apple, A41 WWW, A42 productivity paradox, A43 Xerox PARC, A44 dumb-pipe). Analogy breaks: CUDA moat (V6) may be stickier than a transistor patent; and open weights may commoditize the MODEL layer without value pooling anywhere in the stack (#92, #98).

variables.name: Demand realization / killer-app ROI
variables.current_reading: Unresolved and structurally uncertain — no identified killer app yet generates enough revenue-per-token to underwrite 2027-2030 infrastructure (#23); 'good-enough' enterprise ceiling may cap willingness-to-pay at current capability (#94); benchmark saturation is the near-term leading indicator (#102). Offsetting: defense/deterrence demand floor (#29, #54) and option-value framing (#57). Low-medium confidence either way.
variables.trajectory: Slow to resolve, and a threshold not a continuous variable (#51): the buildout may complete before the killer app arrives (A4 fiber, A42 productivity paradox 20-30yr dynamo-delay compressed to maybe 3-5yr for software). Jevons could expand demand (#47) or algorithmic efficiency collapse it (#13). Timing mismatch: 2026-28 delivery may arrive as training demand peaks into an inference-commoditized market (#103).
variables.base_rate: Infra buildouts routinely overshoot demand timing — real in direction, wrong in timing (A4 fiber, A1 railroads, A12 dreadnought: both sides build, neither wins decisively, value migrates to next paradigm). Productivity gains lag infra by decades and accrue to reorganizers not builders (A42). Analogy breaks: AI has commercial revenue sustaining it that dreadnoughts/Apollo lacked (A9/A12).

edges.note: EUV/tooling is the gate behind the fab gate — no sub-5nm without ASML+Zeiss; this is why China's V1 is stuck (#110, #112, #121, A38/A55).
CoWoS packaging + HBM are the actual production bottleneck for H100/B200-class supply, more binding than wafer starts (#14, #115).
Frontier TRAINING requires leading-edge dense clusters; V1 gates the frontier-model layer but NOT inference/deployment, which run on mature nodes (#9, #109).

Algorithmic efficiency + open weights + inference-time scaling relieve the leading-edge chip constraint, mainly for inference — a chokepoint whose product becomes less necessary loses value (#111, #116, A25/A48).
China controls the materials + grid-equipment (transformers #132, gallium/magnets #113) the US buildout needs at the very first step — a counter-chokepoint on V2 biting before the fab layer.

The two levers are asymmetric in KIND and mutual: V5 (US/allied) PREVENTS China from frontier chips (hard, eroding ~4yr); V4 (China) RAISES the cost/timeline of the Western buildout (soft, durable) — mutual assured disruption, neither a knockout (#114).
CUDA + NVLink/InfiniBand interconnect gate usable large-cluster training even where FLOPS exist — Ascend clusters hit a scaling wall on interconnect, not peak compute (#122, #123).
Frontier model creation is talent-gated where knowledge is tacit; the model layer's value accrues wherever the researchers are, independent of where compute sits (#82, #89, A63).
US deployment is execution-gated — money cannot clear an interconnection queue (#106); grid throughput caps how much demand can actually be served regardless of ROI.
Demand realization underwrites the whole capital structure; a demand miss triggers the fragile-tier cascade (neocloud/SPV) while hyperscalers absorb it (#40, #41, #43, A4).
The actor-specific binding constraints are complementary, not shared: China's stave is V1(chips), the US's is V2(grid) — 'physical constraint' is a vector, not one variable (#108).

Open-weight commoditization + API access undermine both export controls and the CUDA/frontier rent thesis — value may evaporate from the model layer rather than pool at the platform layer (#92, #96, #98, #143).

Gulf/sovereign sub-market capital + energy abundance can relieve the grid-execution constraint by hosting compute where power is unqueued (#18, #147, A88/A11) — but risks landlord-not-owner value capture (A87-A92).

The demand question and commoditization interact: if the model layer commoditizes AND no killer app appears, value migrates to the fragmented application/reorg layer that no subsidy can target (#98, A42).

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will SMIC (or another PRC fab) achieve volume production of a 5nm-class logic node at commercially viable yield (>50%) by end-2028?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Public teardown/analyst confirmation (TechInsights or equivalent) of a PRC-fabbed sub-5nm-class chip in a shipping product at reported yield >50% before 2028-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will US large-power-transformer lead times for datacenter interconnection remain above 2 years as of end-2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Industry surveys (Wood Mackenzie / DOE / utility IRP filings) report median >100MVA transformer lead times >24 months as of 2027-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will China impose a new export restriction on a critical AI-buildout material (gallium, germanium, rare-earth magnets, or graphite) between now and end-2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: MOFCOM/customs announcement of a new or tightened licensing/export control on any listed material after 2026-07-10 and before 2027-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a PRC-indigenous EUV tool reach reported production-worthy status (usable overlay + >100 wph throughput) by end-2028?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Credible reporting (SMIC/SMEE disclosure or supply-chain analyst) of an indigenous EUV tool in HVM use at >100 wafers/hour before 2028-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a non-CUDA accelerator stack (AMD ROCm, or any Chinese CANN-class stack) exceed 20% of new large-scale training deployments globally by end-2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Analyst estimate (Omdia/SemiAnalysis) of >20% share of newly-provisioned training FLOPS on non-CUDA stacks for calendar 2027.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will the US materially liberalize H-1B caps or employment-based green cards for AI/STEM workers before end-2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Enacted federal legislation or rule raising the H-1B cap, creating a STEM cap exemption, or clearing EB green-card backlogs, effective before 2027-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will at least one leveraged neocloud (GPU-backed borrower) default, restructure, or breach a debt covenant by end-2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Public disclosure of a payment default, covenant waiver/breach, or Chapter 11 by a GPU-collateralized neocloud before 2027-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a frontier-competitive open-weight model (within ~6 months of the top closed model on major benchmarks) be released by end-2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: An openly downloadable model within 5 points of the leading closed model on MMLU-Pro/GPQA-class benchmarks, released before 2027-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will aggregate hyperscaler AI capex growth decelerate (YoY growth below +10%) in any guidance issued before end-2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Combined big-4 hyperscaler forward capex guidance shows YoY growth <10% in any quarterly guidance issued before 2027-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a Taiwan-Strait event materially disrupt TSMC leading-edge output before end-2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Any PRC military/coast-guard action causing a reported >2-week halt or >20% cut to TSMC sub-5nm output before 2027-12-31.

binding_constraint_by_actor.reading: Minimum stave is V2 (power & grid execution) — transformers, interconnection queue, parts — NOT capital (#105, #106, #108). Secondary, slower-clearing stave is V7 (talent), the one constraint the US inflicts on itself via immigration policy (#83). Not chip-gated but carries the Taiwan tail-risk in V1 (#118). Read from the graph: the V4→V2 chokepoint and V2→V10 gate both point at execution as the load-bearing edge.

Minimum stave is V1 (leading-edge logic) for FRONTIER TRAINING, gated behind V5 (EUV/tooling) and near-term V3 (packaging/HBM), plus V6 (interconnect scaling wall) (#108, #109, #122). NOT energy-, capital-, speed-, or materials-gated (#107). Partially relieved by V9 (efficiency/open weights) for inference — so China's true binding constraint is narrower than 'chips': frontier-training silicon specifically.

No leading-edge stave it controls, but holds a de-facto veto node in V5 (ASML/Netherlands + Zeiss/Germany, #121). Its own binding constraint for capability is V7 (talent, repelled by GDPR research friction #90) and V10/demand — it lacks a domestic anchor-tenant hyperscaler (#72) and spreads subsidies against agglomeration logic (#76). Landlord-without-tenant risk.

Holds a genuine V2 advantage (cheap unqueued power #147) and V8 sub-market capital (#18), positioning it as a swing inference-supplier by 2030. But its binding constraint is value capture: no V1/V5/V6/V7 — high 'landlord in a tenant economy' risk (A78/A87-A92); dependent on US export-control posture (A90 Eurodollar, A92 Puerto Rico).

summary: The graph is not a single race with one binding input — it is a set of actor-specific minimum staves connected by mutual chokepoints, with a diffusion clock running underneath. China is gated at leading-edge logic fabrication (V1), itself gated by the EUV/tooling chokepoint (V5) and near-term by packaging/HBM (V3); the US is gated not by capital (abundant, #105) but by power-and-grid execution (V2), which China in turn taxes through its control of materials refining and grid-equipment (V4, #113/#132) — so each side holds a chokepoint on the other (#114: mutual assured disruption, not destruction). Above the physical layer sits a value-capture question the physical winners may not control: software/platform lock-in (V6) and open-weight commoditization (V9) can route rents to whoever owns the interface or the reorganized application layer, independent of who owns the fabs or datacenters (smile-curve, #70/#80; A39/A41/A42/A44). The honest read is a CONTESTED race whose direction depends on relative clearing speed on a ~2-4yr clock (#117): the US chip/tooling/software edge is a depreciating asset (frontier diffuses fast: A17/A48), while China's materials lever and the US grid constraint are slower-clearing but not knockouts — and the Taiwan concentration in V1 is the one discontinuity that dwarfs everything else if it triggers (#118, A45/A46/A57).
